title: fix: Change plan manager prompt in order to avoid calling tools directly
body:
# Description

Related to #2777 
在默认plan manager prompt中加入了对tool直接调用的严格限制

# How Has This Been Tested?

经过对话测试，plan manager 成功避免直接调用 Tools

![final](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/c7a3095f-1b34-4495-a597-15d6dfebda8d)

# Snapshots:

![en prompt](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/3d8cac34-7764-4461-8ca8-964a0099b9ef)
![cnprompt](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/b6896063-382d-4203-b194-27e1f4ebc357)


# Checklist:

- [x] My code follows the style guidelines of this project
- [x] I have already rebased the commits and make the commit message conform to the project standard.
- [x] I have performed a self-review of my own code
- [x] I have commented my code, particularly in hard-to-understand areas
- [x] I have made corresponding changes to the documentation
- [x] Any dependent changes have been merged and published in downstream modules


Explore the source code to identify the files and functions to modify.

step_plan.summary: Tighten plan manager prompts to prohibit direct tool calls by the LLM and route tool usage through a dedicated ToolAssistantAgent, updating prompts and example usage to reflect the new delegation policy.

steps.operation: Augment default plan prompt with explicit delegation rule in dashboard prompt
steps.reason: Introduce a strict policy in the plan prompt that if a tool is needed, the plan must delegate to ToolAssistantAgent rather than invoking the tool directly, ensuring separation of concerns and preventing leakage of internal tool-calling behavior into planning prompts.
steps.side_effects: Prompts and downstream planning behavior will now consistently emit delegation steps; existing tests that expect direct tool calls may fail and need updates; downstream components using the plan output may need to route tool usage through ToolAssistantAgent.

steps.operation: Augment default plan prompt with explicit delegation rule in normal chat prompt
steps.reason: Ensure consistent enforcement of the delegation policy across planning flows by updating the normal chat planning prompt to include the same delegation constraint as the dashboard prompt.
steps.side_effects: Consistency across plan generation flows improves, but may require updates to tests and documentation; potential changes in how plans are interpreted by UI or services that assume direct tool calls.

steps.operation: Update example usage to align with new delegation policy and ensure prompt is used
steps.reason: Make sure the AutoPlanChatManager initializes with the updated plan prompt that enforces delegation to ToolAssistantAgent, and adjust usage to reflect the new delegation pattern rather than direct tool invocation.
steps.side_effects: Integration tests referencing direct tool calls may need updates; user-facing demos should reflect the delegation workflow; potential changes in API surfaces for how prompts are passed to the manager.
